João Brito

Journal

Software Engineer at JPMorgan Chase and Information Engineering student at UFABC. Passionate about building robust backend logic, distributed systems, and scalable cloud infrastructure.

Prior experience includes designing automated data platforms and optimizing ETL pipelines at Itaú Unibanco. Certified AWS Cloud Practitioner and HashiCorp Terraform Associate. This journal serves as a repository of insights on software architecture, cloud engineering, and backend systems.

Articles

  • July 6, 2026

    Building my home server: from native chaos to a dockerized media empire

    A self-hosted media stack is less about the content it serves and more about the containerization, networking, and automation patterns you are forced to get right.

  • July 6, 2026

    Building Itinera: The Reality of Going Local First

    Offline-first application architecture is less about database choice and more about designing for conflict, security origin inheritance, and silent authentication proxying.

  • July 6, 2026

    Standardizing Code Style Across Multi-Project Environments

    Why automated style enforcement is an architectural decision, not a preference, and how shifting formatting checks left changes the engineering culture around code review.
